---
title: "Quest 7: HashMaps - Keys and Values"
subtitle: "Store and Look Up Data Quickly"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🗝️ QUEST 7 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/07-dictionaries.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/07-dictionaries.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Need to look up data by name? Use a `HashMap`.

## 💡 Basic HashMap

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

fn main() {
    let mut hp = HashMap::new();
    hp.insert("slime", 8);
    hp.insert("wolf", 20);

    println!("{:?}", hp);
}
```

## 👨‍💻 Example Lookup

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

fn main() {
    let mut prices = HashMap::new();
    prices.insert("apple", 2);
    prices.insert("banana", 1);

    if let Some(cost) = prices.get("apple") {
        println!("Apple costs {}", cost);
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Create a map of 3 school subjects and your confidence level (1-5). Print one lookup.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

fn main() {
    let mut confidence = HashMap::new();
    confidence.insert("math", 4);
    confidence.insert("science", 3);
    confidence.insert("history", 5);

    if let Some(level) = confidence.get("science") {
        println!("Science confidence: {}", level);
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 7 Complete
You created, inserted, and looked up values in a `HashMap`.
Next: [Quest 8: Fibonacci](08-fibonacci.qmd)
:::
